title: Calibrating 10-AU fluorometer to quantify extracted chl-a
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"d8dd7","text":"This method is for use for calibrating the 10-AU flurometer using chl-a extracts. The method also provides guidelines for extracting chl-a and calculating absorbance using a spectrophotometer, making dilutions of extracts that can be used to create differetn chl-a concentrations","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5sd0l","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"73v8i","text":"Fixed Application can be found under \"general tests\" on the spectrophotometer. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4dl63","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dep7t","text":"Make sure the cuvette is in the correct position for measurements. Misplacement may cause an error such as a negative absorbance measurement.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7r3og","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dsqa8","text":"Spinach only has chlorophyll a. If using an organism that has more pigments, will have to subtract those from total absorbance (i.e. chlorophyll a - chlorophyll c).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4kghq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5qa9g","text":"See MSDS safety and hazard warnings.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"3madv","text":"97% acetone","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fog8g","text":"spinach or green plant as source for chlorophyll extracts","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Extract chlorophyll from 1 g of gound spinach with 5 mL of 90% acetone at -20 °C overnight.

Use the Biomate 5 spectrophotometer to measure the adsorption of extracted chlorophyll by adding 1 mL to a quartz cuvette. Make sure the cuvette is in the correct position in the machine. Zero the machine by blanking with 1 mL of 90% acetone. Measure "multi λ" with λ1 = 664 nm and λ2 = 647 nm. (Jeffrey 1975)

Calculate the concentration of diluted chlorophyll extract using the absorbance values from the spectrophotometer and the equation: concentration of chlorophyll a = 11.93(λ1) - 1.93(λ2) (Jeffrey 1975) If using an organism that has more pigments, will have to subtract those from total absorbance (i.e. chlorophyll a - chlorophyll c). The spinach we used did not require subtraction of chlorophyll c. Example: 11.93(0.736) - 1.93(0.479) = 7.85601 ug/ml
Convert answer from go to step #4 to ug/L, because this is the readout for the fluorometer.
Turn on the 10-AU Fluorometer and allow the instrument to warm up for at least 30 minutes.

Based on the absorbance value from go to step #2 : dilute the chlorophyll extract so that the absorbance is in optimum range for the fluorometer. Optimum rangle is Abs λ1 and λ2= 0.3-0.85. Example: Undiluted Abs λ1= 1.88 Abs λ2= 1.324 20-fold dilution: Abs λ1= 0.736 Abs λ2= 0.479

Create solutions of varying concentrations to test the fluorometer readings. Use C1V1=C2V2 to make solutions at different concentrations based on expected field concentrations, for example: 20, 10, 5, 1 and 0.5 ug/L. Also, create an acetone blank (with no chlorophyll). For example: 7,856 ug/L (C1) For a 5 ug/L concentration (C2) in 7 mls acetone (V2) (7,856 ug/L)(V1)=(5 ug/L)(7 ml) V1= 4.45519 ug

Use the concentration most expected in the field to set as the solid standard. Table 1 is an example of how to set the sensitivity scale for the 10-AU. Use the table to determine the appropriate range and %FS for the calibration standard.
Clear past calibrations on screen #2.6.
Access screen #2.43 and set range control to "manual" and range to the appropriate level for the standard chosen. For example, we set range control to "Man" and "Med."
Access screen #3.2. Loosen the sensitivity locking screw using an allen wrench. (The sensitivity lock is the small screw to the left of the number pad.)

Insert and cover your selected standard concentration. Use a coin or tool to slowly turn the sensitivity knob (located to the right of the number pad, under the power button) to adjust the %FS to the value appropriate for the standard concentration according to Table 1 in go to step #8 . A deviation of 5% less than or greater than the table is acceptable. When %FS reaches the designated value, tighten the sensitivity lock. At this point, sensitivity is set. Example: For 5 ug/L. FS = 21%
Access screen #2.43 again, and set range control to "auto."

Access screen #2.1. Insert and cover the acetone blank created in go to step #7 . Press "1" to Run Blank. Wait for the reading to stabilize for about 8 seconds, and then press "0." The instrument will take 15 seconds to complete blanking. At this point, blank is set.
Calibrate with your standard:
Access screen #2.2 and input the actual concentration of your standard. Return to screen #2.0
Insert and cover the standard.
Run standard by pressing "3." Wait about 8 seconds for reading to stabilize.
Press the "*" to set the calibration point. After about 15 seconds, a message with appear that says "Finished".
Test the 10-AU fluorometer using the concetrations created in go to step #7 to assure appropriate readout. Example: Expected Observed 5 ug/L 5.01 ug/L 1 ug/L 1.11 ug/L Blank 0.0 ug/L
creator: Shelby Whitehead
